dataset: NACA 4-Digit Airfoil Series (Real aeronautical standard data)

pi_items.verified_value: NACA 4415 (real standardized airfoil from National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics)
pi_items.source: NACA airfoil nomenclature system verified against multiple aeronautical references

NACA 4415 nomenclature: last two digits (15) represent 15% maximum thickness. Validated via generated coordinates and verified against NACA 0015 (actual airfoil data: 0.1500) and NACA 4412 (actual airfoil data: 0.1202)
Derived from base thickness 0.15 scaled by factor 25/15 = 1.6667. Mathematical verification confirms 0.15 × (25/15) = 0.25 exactly
Derived from base thickness 0.15 scaled by factor 12/15 = 0.8. Mathematical verification confirms 0.15 × (12/15) = 0.12 exactly

Tolerance testing with real NACA airfoil data: NACA 0015 calculated as 0.1500 (error 0.000040), NACA 4412 calculated as 0.1202 (error 0.000200). Verifier uses 1% chord binning method. Tolerance of ±0.01 (1% chord) is appropriate for airfoil thickness measurement accuracy

pi_items.metadata_value: bin-based maximum thickness from upper/lower surface difference
pi_items.verified_value: Bin-width 0.01 (1% chord), checked in region 0.1 < x < 0.9, thickness = max(y_upper) - min(y_lower) per x-bin
pi_items.source: Verified against actual NACA 0015 airfoil data (35 coordinate points) producing 0.1500 thickness with 0.000040 error
install_qblade.sh line 71 specifies QBlade v0.96.2 64-bit for Linux. QBlade v0.96 documented as supporting NACA 4-digit airfoil generation via built-in NACA Generator

pi_items.metadata_value: National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics standard
pi_items.verified_value: NACA airfoils are standardized designs from NACA (founded 1915, predecessor of NASA). Nomenclature system is internationally recognized in aeronautical engineering
pi_items.source: NACA airfoil research is foundational aeronautical work. NACA 4-digit system allows exact specification: digit 1 = max camber (%), digit 2 = camber position (10%), digits 3-4 = max thickness (%). NACA 4415 unambiguously specifies 15% thickness at 30% chord location

Task description line 4 specifies export to '/home/ga/Documents/airfoils/family/' with filenames 'base.dat', 'root.dat', and 'tip.dat'. setup_task.sh line 19 creates this directory. Consistent across all task files
Task description line 5 specifies saving QBlade project to '/home/ga/Documents/projects/airfoil_family_scaling.wpa'. export_result.sh line 11 checks this path. Consistent across all task files
Task description line 4 specifies 'base.dat' as the export filename. Task.json metadata line 21 confirms. export_result.sh line 37 checks this file
Task description line 4 specifies 'root.dat' as the export filename. Task.json metadata line 26 confirms. export_result.sh line 38 checks this file
Task description line 4 specifies 'tip.dat' as the export filename. Task.json metadata line 31 confirms. export_result.sh line 39 checks this file

privileged_info_summary: The task uses NACA 4415, a real standardized airfoil from the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics with exactly 15% maximum thickness. The metadata correctly specifies expected thicknesses: base 0.15 (matching NACA 4415 specification), root 0.25 (scaled by 1.6667×), and tip 0.12 (scaled by 0.8×). The tolerance of ±0.01 is appropriate based on verification against actual NACA airfoil coordinate data (NACA 0015 measured 0.1500 ±0.000040, NACA 4412 measured 0.1202 ±0.0002). The thickness calculation method using 1% chord bins in the verifier is mathematically sound. QBlade v0.96.2 supports NACA 4-digit generation. All file paths and names in the metadata are consistent with task specifications and installation scripts. The data is not synthetic—NACA airfoils are real aeronautical standards used in wind turbine blade design and aviation.
